issue_title: Running the parser with one alternative keyword provides much more accurate results than a list of keywords.
issue_body:
The first time I ran the parser I used the following. 

memes = findMemes("meme",Meme_secondary_keywords,i)

Where "findMemes" is simply the parser wrapped in a method, Meme_secondary_keywords is a list of meme related terms like "greg,trump, and kobe" and i is superfluous

The second time I ran the parser I switched to this method

for word in variety_keywords:
     Secondarykeyword = []
     Secondarykeyword.append(word)
     memes = findMemes(search_keyword,Secondarykeyword,i)
     i = i + 1

In the above, i just acts as a tag that is added onto each unique results folder.

The results from the second method contained FAR more "meme-like" content. Reviewing the results from the first method, I noticed that after the first 50 or so results I was getting very few memes. 

For example, the word "Trump" is the 11th element of the list, so it will run after 1,100 searches have been done. The first method of running the method did not return any trump memes, all of the results were just generic pictures of trump. The second method returned over 95 pictures which I would classify as memes

This is likely an idiosyncrasy of google's image search algorithm. I would hypothesize that as you get further from the original results page the scope of the results widens dramatically. I do not know if this problem can be fixed on your end, but I think it would be useful for end users to be aware of the second method. If I had ran my parse with the first method my results would be unusable, but the second method gave me very good data

issue_title: `determine_[de]serializer_for_data` is confusingly named
issue_body:
Terminology in GenericField:
“deserializer” —> a thing that turns your model into a json blob
“serializer” —> a thing that turns your json blob into a model

These seem backward to me. I don't know exactly what the fix should be. Merely switching them could be a bit of a confusing upgrade path.

What about renaming them at the same time? `determine` could be shortened to `get`. Then we could leave fallbacks in place for the old names, with deprecation warnings.

Perhaps for extra clarity, 'data' on the outgoing serializer could be 'instance', signalling that you're serializing a model instance.

* `determine_deserializer_for_data` -> `get_serializer_for_instance`
* `determine_serializer_for_data` -> `get_deserializer_for_data`

Another alternative:
* `determine_deserializer_for_data` -> `get_outgoing_serializer`
* `determine_serializer_for_data` -> `get_incoming_serializer`

issue_title: Daphne server fails silently if the port is already in use
issue_body:
**Summary**: If the port is already in use, daphne pretends to be running, but doesn't respond to requests.

**Expected behaviour**: daphne should fail with an error message when the port is already in use.

I experienced this behaviour when I was running `./manage.py runserver` twice (my mistake). The first instance did respond, while the second one didn't. Even after terminating the first instance, the second one didn't react. 

Same behaviour with running `daphne -b 127.0.0.1 -p 8000 config.asgi:channel_layer` twice. That's why I'm reporting this in the `daphne` repo and not in `channels`.

It's not critical, but unexpected. Would be happy to support you in solving this.

issue_body:
Hello,

I am implementing a neural network in MXNetR. I attempted to customize my loss function to compute the correlation between my output vector and the targeting vector. Below is my code:

Below is my code:
```
    # Generate testing data
    train.x = matrix(data = rexp(200, rate = 10), nrow = 120, ncol = 6380)
    test.x = matrix(data = rexp(200, rate = 10), nrow = 60, ncol = 6380)
    train.y = matrix(data = rexp(200, rate = 10), nrow = 120, ncol = 319)
    test.y = matrix(data = rexp(200, rate = 10), nrow = 60, ncol = 319)
   
    # Reshape testing data
    train.array <-train.x
    dim(train.array) <-c(20,319,1,ncol(train.x))
    test.array<-test.x
    dim(test.array) <-c (20,319,1,ncol(test.x))

    # Define the input data
    data <- mx.symbol.Variable("data")
    
    # Define the first fully connected layer
    fc1 <- mx.symbol.FullyConnected(data, num_hidden = 100)
    act.fun <- mx.symbol.Activation(fc1, act_type = "relu") # create a hidden layer with Rectified Linear Unit as its activation function.
    output <<- mx.symbol.FullyConnected(act.fun, num_hidden = 319)
    
    # Customize loss function
    label <- mx.symbol.Variable("label")
   
    output_mean <- mx.symbol.mean(output)
    label_mean <- mx.symbol.mean(label)

    output_delta <-mx.symbol.broadcast_sub(output, output_mean)
    label_delta <- mx.symbol.broadcast_sub(label, label_mean)

    output_sqr <-mx.symbol.square(output_delta)
    label_sqr <- mx.symbol.square(label_delta)
    
    output_sd <- mx.symbol.sqrt(mx.symbol.sum(output_delta))
    label_sd <- mx.symbol.sqrt(mx.symbol.sum(label_delta))
    
    numerator <- mx.symbol.sum(output_delta * label_delta)
    denominator <- output_sd * label_sd

    lro <- mx.symbol.MakeLoss(numerator/denominator)

    # Generate a new model
    model <- mx.model.FeedForward.create(symbol=lro, X=train.array, y=train.y, 
                                     num.round=5000, array.batch.size=1, optimizer = "adam",
                                     learning.rate = 0.0003, eval.metric = mx.metric.rmse,
                                     epoch.end.callback = mx.callback.log.train.metric(20, logger))
```

issue_title: difficulty replicating layers with non-visible outputs (e.g. Dropout) with python CustomOp
issue_body:
I'm trying to replicate existing layers in python (the goal is to modify layers, initially in python), and am having particular difficulty with layers that hold onto information from training, such as the mask in Dropout. Is this possible with CustomOp? For example, in mxnet/src/operator/dropout-inl.h, two outputs are created, with the second output being the mask used for dropping out connections. This second output is then used in backprop so the right weights are used. Naively, a python layer for Dropout would assign a mask of indices to the second output, like so:
`self.assign(out_data[1], req[0], mx.nd.array(~indices))`
The trouble with CustomOp is it does not take into account that we only want one output to go on to the next layer, so the bind will fail if I add a second output naively in my CustomOp. 

In C/C++ mxnet, this is handled by the Dropout class implementing a member function for NumVisibleOutputs() to return 1, and NumOutputs() to return 2.  What is the right way to do this in the python CustomOp, or is the only way at present to use an auxiliary output to hold it?

`class Dropout(mx.operator.CustomOp):
    def __init__(self, keep):
        self.keep = float(keep)
        self.drop = 1 - self.keep
        self.scale = 1.0/self.keep
        self.is_train=True
        super(Dropout, self).__init__()
    def forward(self, is_train, req, in_data, out_data, aux):
        x = in_data[0].asnumpy()
        # if we're in training stage, drop out randomly, otherwise, don't drop anything
        if (is_train):
            self.keep_idx = np.random.rand(*x.shape) < self.keep
            self.is_train=True
            x[~keep_idx] = 0
            self.assign(out_data[0], req[0], mx.nd.array(x*self.scale))
            self.assign(out_data[1], req[0], mx.nd.array(keep_idx))
        else:
            self.is_train=False
            self.assign(out_data[0], req[0], mx.nd.array(x))
    def backward(self, req, out_grad, in_data, out_data, in_grad, aux):
        y = out_grad[0].asnumpy()
        keep_idx=out_data[1].asnumpy()
        if (self.is_train==True):
            y[~keep_idx]=0
            self.assign(in_grad[0], req[0], mx.nd.array(y*self.scale))
        else:
            self.assign(in_grad[0], req[0], mx.nd.array(y))`

`@mx.operator.register("replicated_dropout")
class DropoutProp(mx.operator.CustomOpProp):
    def __init__(self, keep):
        self.keep = keep
        super(DropoutProp, self).__init__(need_top_grad=True)

    def list_arguments(self):
        return ['data']

    def list_outputs(self):
        #return ['output']
        return ['data', 'mask']

    def infer_shape(self, in_shape):
        data_shape = in_shape[0]
        output_shape = in_shape[0]
        aux_shape = in_shape[0]
        return [data_shape], [output_shape, output_shape], []

    def infer_type(self, in_type):
        dtype = in_type[0]
        return [dtype], [dtype, dtype], []

    def create_operator(self, ctx, shapes, dtypes):
        print('creating Dropout operator')
        return Dropout(self.keep)

net_stock = mx.sym.Dropout(data=net, name='stock_drop', p=0.5)
net_custom = mx.symbol.Custom(data=net, name='custom_drop', op_type='replicated_dropout', keep=0.5)`

issue_title: mxnet.ndarray.khatri_rao needs GPU version
issue_body:
## Description
1. khatri_rao was prematurely added to mx.nd/mx.sym namespace instead of contrib namespace which happened in https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/pull/7781/files#r176510540.
2. khatri_rao doesn't have GPU version.

Given that this PR was merged last year, this feature has made its way into releases already. To resolve, we need to do one of the following:
1. mark the released op as deprecated and move it back to contrib in the next major version.
2. move forward: a) one more pass of vetting on the API design (cc @piiswrong), and then 2) implement the GPU version.

cc @cswiercz

issue_title: Management command revamp for argparse broken commands
issue_labels: [{'id': 77993, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/django-extensions/django-extensions/labels/bug', 'name': 'bug', 'color': 'ff0000', 'default': True}, {'id': 1003471, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/django-extensions/django-extensions/labels/need%20patch', 'name': 'need patch', 'color': 'd7e102', 'default': False}, {'id': 30635832, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/django-extensions/django-extensions/labels/need%20test%20case', 'name': 'need test case', 'color': 'ededed', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
`runserver_plus` no longer accepts the positional argument of `0.0.0.0:8000` (ip address/port combo) because the changes in this pull request ignore positional arguments. It looks like `optparse` was forgiving with regards to positional arguments, just passing them through to be handled. `argparse` expects them to be defined, it seems.  This is directly related to #820. The PR that causes this (mis)behaviour is #804 (I think).

See Django `master`'s current runserver code for this positional argument: https://github.com/django/django/blob/master/django/core/management/commands/runserver.py#L39

This is the error I see when using django-extensions `master` branch:

```
(myproject)vagrant@vagrant-ubuntu-trusty-64:/vagrant$ python manage.py runserver_plus 0.0.0.0:8000
usage: manage.py runserver_plus [-h] [--version] [-v {0,1,2,3}]
                                [--settings SETTINGS]
                                [--pythonpath PYTHONPATH] [--traceback]
                                [--no-color] [--ipv6] [--noreload] [--browser]
                                [--adminmedia ADMIN_MEDIA_PATH]
                                [--nothreading] [--threaded]
                                [--output OUTPUT_FILE] [--print-sql]
                                [--cert CERT_PATH] [--extra-file EXTRA_FILES]
                                [--reloader-interval RELOADER_INTERVAL]
                                [--pdb] [--ipdb] [--pm]
                                [--startup-messages STARTUP_MESSAGES]
                                [--nostatic] [--insecure]
manage.py runserver_plus: error: unrecognized arguments: 0.0.0.0:8000
```

I suspect that other django-extensions' management commands will need a positional argument review as well - particularly when you see other related issues like #820.

These repositories are in a `ext4` partition. Is this filesystem not supported by watchman?

issue_title: Configurable validates too often
issue_body:
Consider this:

```python
from traitlets.config import Configurable
class A(Configurable):
    x = Int(1)
    
    @validate('x')
    def f(self, proposal):
        print('validate x')
        return proposal.value
A(x=5)
``` 
prints:
```
validate x
validate x
```

while this:

```python
from traitlets import HasTraits
class A(HasTraits):
    x = Int(1)

    @validate('x')
    def f(self, proposal):
        print('validate x')
        return proposal.value
A(x=5)
```
only validates x once:
```
validate x
```
For some reason, Configurable is validating x twice, but HasTraits is only validating once.

issue_title: Workers dying on errors that can't be unpickled
RemoteTarget creates a non-existant file/directory, but its test expects a raised error
issue_body:
Hey folks -

I was taking a look at some tests, when I saw that on Travis, one particular test that is testing `luigi/contrib/ssh.py` [fails but the build still succeeds](https://travis-ci.org/spotify/luigi/jobs/101167651#L308). I thought I'd fix this, since I assumed that creating a temp file in `/etc` was the issue by just using Python's `tempfile` lib.

However, digging through `luigi/contrib/ssh.py:RemoteTarget`, it looks like the creation of the `file_writer` creates the parents' directories automatically (and then for the actual file to be created if it's not already there). So that leads me to be a bit confused that the associated test [luigi/contrib/test_ssh.py](https://github.com/spotify/luigi/blob/master/test/contrib/test_ssh.py#L195-L200) expects `RemoteCalledProcessError` to be raised, when it's actually not.

Which would be the preferred behavior?

issue_title: 3.3 release problems
issue_body:
I noticed you have several problems:
* ASSIMP_VERSION_MINOR isn't updated to 3: CMakeLists.txt:SET (ASSIMP_VERSION_MINOR 2)
* pstdint.h isn't of the latest version. You have 0.1.12 and the current version is 0.1.15.4. The FreeBSD port for some reason patches it to 0.1.15.4. I am not sure why, somebody needed the latest version. But why don't you just update it in here, upstream? Here is the latest version: http://www.azillionmonkeys.com/qed/pstdint.h
* code/BlenderTessellator.cpp fails to compile with clang, and requires this patch:
```
 #define OffsetOf( Class, Member ) ( static_cast< unsigned int >( \
-    reinterpret_cast<uint8_t*>(&( reinterpret_cast< Class* >( NULL )->*( &Class::Member ) )) - \
+    reinterpret_cast<uint8_t*>(&( reinterpret_cast< Class* >( 0 )->*( &Class::Member ) )) - \
     static_cast<uint8_t*>(NULL) ) )
```

Could you please consider fixing these and making another release?
Thank you.

issue_title: head_object hangs and fails when querying SSE-C encrypted object
issue_body:
I am auditing many tens of millions of S3 objects for a compliance related project.

Using python 2.7.9 & boto3 1.6.3

This involves in some cases calling head_object on an object with SSE-C encryption without the proper SSE-C key.


If I do this using get_object:

objectMetadata = s3_client.get_object(Bucket = bucketName, Key = object.key)


I get the expected error:

<class 'botocore.exceptions.ClientError'>
---
ClientError(u'An error occurred (InvalidRequest) when calling the GetObject operation: The object was stored using a form of Server Side Encryption. The correct parameters must be provided to retrieve the object.',)



querying exactly the same key with head_object:

objectMetadata = s3_client.head_object(Bucket = bucketName, Key = object.key)

I get this after a several minutes long delay:

<class 'botocore.exceptions.ClientError'>
---
ClientError(u'An error occurred (500) when calling the HeadObject operation (reached max retries: 14381): Internal Server Error',)
---

all of this is after a simple client setup:

    s3_client = boto3.client('s3',
            aws_access_key_id = awsAccessKey,
            aws_secret_access_key = awsSecretKey,
            region_name='us-east-1',
            config=botocore.client.Config(signature_version='s3v4', retries = dict(max_attempts = 2)))



looking at CloudTrail access logs, im surprised to see lots of records with:

"eventName":"HeadObject","errorCode":"InvalidRequest","errorMessage":"The object was stored using a form of Server Side Encryption. The correct parameters must be provided to retrieve the object."

it seems like boto3 is unable to properly interpret this error condition of reading SSE-C encrypted objects without getting the encryption key with the request.

issue_title: d
s3 config with use_accelerate_endpoint and generate_presigned_post does not work
issue_body:
This code:

```
    s3 = boto3.client('s3', config=Config(s3={'use_accelerate_endpoint': True}))
    s3.generate_presigned_post(
        Bucket = 'some bucket,
        Key = 'some key'

...will not result in the correct url being generated for a presigned post operation.  The bug is in this code, in `client.py`:

```
        # Enable accelerate if the configuration is set to to true or the

        if s3_accelerate or S3_ACCELERATE_ENDPOINT in self._endpoint.host:

            self.meta.events.register_first(

```

...`request-created.s3` is never called for a presigned post, because no request to s3 is made.

issue_title: Support for Pymemcache
issue_body:
I noticed that DD APM didn't pick up memcached calls in our application while NR APM did. It seems this is really down to the fact that the datadog integration [explicitly patches pylibmc](https://github.com/DataDog/dd-trace-py/blob/master/ddtrace/contrib/pylibmc/patch.py#L1) while the new relic integration [generically wraps the module](https://github.com/edmorley/newrelic-python-agent/blob/master/newrelic/newrelic/hooks/datastore_memcache.py#L65). 

I took a quick look and it seems that pymemcache supports the same methods that pylibmc does and the `**kwargs` in the client wrapper used by DataDog can pass the additional arguments down without issue. 

I think this would be an easy add, although I'm not quite sure how we could patch the client through django settings. 🤔

issue_title: issue with diff method
issue_body:
I'm using gitpython to collect diff information between a commit and his parent.
Generally, the following code works fine when the number of diffs to retrieve is small:
```diffs = c.parents[0].diff(c, create_patch=True)```
Conversely, when the number of diffs is huge (https://git.eclipse.org/c/papyrus/org.eclipse.papyrus.git/commit/?id=f5f817279baa2008450aa32b18e576c2fcda02bb), that code is not able to produce an output after 24h (at least).
Is there another way I could use to retrieve the diff information between two commits?

Below you can find the code to replicate this behaviour:
```python
from git import *

REPO_PATH = ""C:/Users/.../org.eclipse.papyrus"" (you can clone it from here: https://git.eclipse.org/c/papyrus/org.eclipse.papyrus.git/)

BRANCH = "2.0.0"

def main():
    repo = Repo(REPO_PATH, odbt=GitCmdObjectDB)
    reference = [r for r in repo.references if r.name == BRANCH][0]
    for c in repo.iter_commits(rev=reference):
        if c.hexsha == 'f5f817279baa2008450aa32b18e576c2fcda02bb':
            diffs = c.parents[0].diff(c, create_patch=True)
            print str(len(diffs))
            break

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

issue_title: Expose a way to serialize/deserialize headers and body for different arg schemes
issue_body:
Now that we support fallback endpoints, it is possible to implement endpoints that work with multiple arg schemes. It would be nice if we provided some way to take the raw bytes of the header or the body from the request and deserialize them based on whatever arg scheme request is using, and do the reverse for raw bytes of the header/body of the response.

Currently it's possible to use `JsonSerializer` and `ThriftSerializer` directly but they're not part of the public API and shouldn't be exposed as-is.

issue_title: Quickstart and admin user
issue_body:
I am trying out NSOT inside an Ubuntu 16.04 lxd container.  I have installed python2 and the other [requirements](http://nsot.readthedocs.io/en/latest/install/ubuntu.html#installation). I have created a virtualenv but otherwise am following [the quickstart](http://nsot.readthedocs.io/en/latest/quickstart.html) guide.

```
virtualenv venv
. venv/bin/activate
pip install nsot
nsot-server init
# Edit ~/.nsot/nsot.conf.py to set NSOT_HOST = '0.0.0.0'
nsot-server start
```

All appears to be fine: it applies all the migrations and starts the webserver, and I can connect.

The problem is that although the Quick Start guide says to "create a superuser when prompted", there is no such prompt.  I therefore wonder whether some initialisation step has been missed?

I can [create one manually](http://nsot.readthedocs.io/en/latest/admin.html#create-a-superuser) and it seems to be OK.  Is this just a step missing from the Quick Start?

self = <hal_impl.i2c_helpers.I2CSimBase object at 0x10dc90d68>, port = 0, deviceAddress = 4, dataToSend = <hal.functions.c_ubyte_Array_6 object at 0x10dc6f488>, sendSize = 6

issue_title: Installing pytest via requirements.txt on Python 2 does not install required funcsigs
issue_body:
Be sure to check the existing issues, both open and closed.

When installing pytest via an imported requirements.txt on Python2, the extra requirement of funcsigs is not installed.

##### Describe your environment

1. OS: macOS High Sierra 10.13.2
1. Python version: 2.7.13
1. Pipenv version: 9.0.1

##### Expected result

funcsigs should be installed

##### Actual result

funcsigs is not installed

##### Steps to replicate

```
cd $(mktemp -d)
echo pytest > requirements.txt
pipenv --two install -r requirements.txt
```

When running `pipenv graph` the output shows that funcsigs is a dependency, but no version is installed: `funcsigs [required: Any, installed: ?]`.

Note that using the command `pipenv --two install pytest` to install pytest directly instead of via a requirements.txt file installs funcsigs as expected. The dependency is specified in pytest [here](https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/blob/672c901c704384b28e0079209684ba71e8867bf3/setup.py#L58).

issue_title: Since setuptools 28.5.0, a "global-exclude .pyc" no longer matches .pyc files
issue_body:
Since commit bb45468d27615c2ce9f9c9757a367c44d6ee80d2, if a project's MANIFEST.in contains ``global-exclude .py[co]``, the archive generated with ``python setup.py sdist`` will still contain all ``.pyc`` / ``.pyo`` files.

The test below shows that this line worked in setuptools 28.4.0, and broke in setuptools 28.5.0.

I'm not sure whether this can be considered a bug: the previous implementation looked up patterns without any anchoring, whereas the new one forces users to explicitly include wildcards in their ``MANIFEST.in``.
However, I think this change should have been mentioned in the version Changelog.

```patch
diff --cc setuptools/tests/test_manifest.py
index 6360270,ef4beda..0000000
--- a/setuptools/tests/test_manifest.py
+++ b/setuptools/tests/test_manifest.py
@@@ -425,12 -464,22 +425,19 @@@ class TestFileListTest(TempDirTestCase)
          assert file_list.files == ['b.txt']
          self.assertWarnings()
  
+         file_list = FileList()
+         file_list.files = ['a.py', 'b.txt', l('d/c.pyc'), 'e.pyo']
+         file_list.process_template_line('global-exclude .py[co]')
+         file_list.sort()
+         assert file_list.files == ['a.py', 'b.txt']
+         self.assertNoWarnings()
+ 
          # recursive-include
  ```

issue_title: Txmongo is unable to handle more than 1000 objects in bulk operations
issue_body:
Hi. I'm testing the following code using txmongo 16.1.0. Basically, I'm sending 1001 objects to an insert_many.  It's raising pymongo.errors.OperationFailure: TxMongo: command SON ... ('writeConcern', {})]) on namespace foo.$cmd failed with 'exceeded maximum write batch size of 1000'
``` python
import time
import txmongo
from twisted.internet import defer, reactor


@defer.inlineCallbacks
def example():
    mongo = yield txmongo.connection.ConnectionPool('mongodb://127.0.0.1:27018')

    foo = mongo.foo  # `foo` database
    test = foo.test  # `test` collection

    # insert some data
    insert_data = []
    for x in range(1001):
        insert_data.append({"something": x*time.time()})

    result = yield test.insert_many(insert_data)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    example().addCallback(lambda ign: reactor.stop())
    reactor.run()
```
Mongo has a limit of 1000 for Bulk operations, but drivers usually handle this by dividing batch operations. Pymongo does not fail with many more objects.

issue_title: Improper handling of character range `#x10000-#x10FFFF` (UTF16)
issue_body:
According to the [YAML spec](http://yaml.org/spec/1.2/spec.html#Characters) the characters `#x10000-#x10FFFF` do not need to be escaped. 

As a second bug, the characters in this range are being encoded in a way that is not compatible with javascript. For example, that character `🎁` should be represented to javascript as `\uD83C\uDF81` or `\u{1F381}` but is instead being sent as `\U0001F381`.

issue_title: Offset resets while Kafka cluster unavailable.
issue_body:
Hi,
I had a question about the intended behavior of a consumer and the offset while a Kafka cluster is unavailable. 
This scenario happened when I restarted a `kafka` cluster while a consumer was still running. 

Here is the simplified  consumer code (running on pykafka 2.0.4)

```
    consumer = grid_topic.get_balanced_consumer(
        consumer_group="...",
        zookeeper_connect="..",
        auto_commit_enable=False
    )

    while True:
         message = message.consume()
         process(message)
         consumer.commit()
```
Here are the logs during when the kafka cluster was restarting.
```
Dec 17 17:06:01 <ip> docker[2033]: INFO:pykafka.cluster:Attempting to discover offset manager for consumer group 'dumont'
Dec 17 17:06:01 <ip> docker[2033]: INFO:pykafka.cluster:Found coordinator broker with id 1
Dec 17 17:06:01 <ip> docker[2033]: ERROR:pykafka.simpleconsumer:Error fetching offsets for topic grid (errors: {<class 'pykafka.exceptions.OffsetsLoadInProgress'>: [0]})
Dec 17 17:06:01 <ip> docker[2033]: ERROR:pykafka.simpleconsumer:Error fetching offsets for topic grid (errors: {<class 'pykafka.exceptions.OffsetsLoadInProgress'>: [0]})
Dec 17 17:06:02 <ip> docker[2033]: INFO:pykafka.handlers:RequestHandler worker: exiting cleanly
Dec 17 17:06:02 <ip> docker[2033]: INFO:pykafka.handlers:RequestHandler worker: exiting cleanly
Dec 17 17:06:02 <ip> docker[2033]: ERROR:pykafka.simpleconsumer:Error fetching offsets for topic grid (errors: {<class 'pykafka.exceptions.OffsetsLoadInProgress'>: [0]})
Dec 17 17:06:04 <ip> docker[2033]: ERROR:pykafka.simpleconsumer:Error fetching offsets for topic grid (errors: {<class 'pykafka.exceptions.OffsetsLoadInProgress'>: [0]})
Dec 17 17:06:07 <ip> docker[2033]: ERROR:pykafka.simpleconsumer:Error fetching offsets for topic grid (errors: {<class 'pykafka.exceptions.OffsetsLoadInProgress'>: [0]})
Dec 17 17:06:11 <ip> docker[2033]: INFO:pykafka.simpleconsumer:Starting 1 fetcher threads
Dec 17 17:06:11 <ip> docker[2033]: INFO:pykafka.balancedconsumer:Rebalancing Complete.
Dec 17 17:06:11 <ip> docker[2033]: INFO:pykafka.simpleconsumer:Resetting offsets in response to OffsetOutOfRangeError
Dec 17 17:06:11 <ip> docker[2033]: INFO:pykafka.simpleconsumer:Resetting offsets for 1 partitions
Dec 17 17:06:11 <ip> docker[2033]: INFO:pykafka.simpleconsumer:Flushed queue for partition 0
```

This is my current understanding of what is happening behind the scenes, please point out if I've made a mistake! 

This behavior from the logs matches the [code](https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka/blob/e3fb2e95ff29d9bfcb080991ad6dcbf140a71ac9/pykafka/simpleconsumer.py#L494).

It retries to fetch the offset ([5 times, backing off 1000ms * try number each time](https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka/blob/e3fb2e95ff29d9bfcb080991ad6dcbf140a71ac9/pykafka/simpleconsumer.py#L64)) before continuing. 

**After it continues, it assumes the offset cannot be found, and therefore resets it according to the `auto_offset_reset`.**

This behavior doesn't seem ideal (at least in our application). It seems to be able to detect the `OffsetsLoadInProgress` error, would it make sense to raise an Exception if that is the last  error encountered on the retry as the offset should be available afterward? 

It appears the retry does already do something with [`NotCoordinatorForConsumer` and `OffsetsLoadInProgress`]((https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka/blob/e3fb2e95ff29d9bfcb080991ad6dcbf140a71ac9/pykafka/simpleconsumer.py#L518)) exceptions, but I'm not sure what that is.

Happy to provide more details if it's helpful!

issue_title: Pyregion should use distortion-aware WCS functions for coordinate transformations
issue_body:
Currently, `pyregion` uses WCS functions that do not take into account distortion corrections: `wcs.wcs_world2pix()` and `wcs.wcs_pix2world()`. However, distortions for most HST instruments are significant and should be taken into account. This could be done  by switching to  `wcs.all_world2pix()` and `wcs.all_pix2world()` - really a very simple change to the code.

While at this moment I cannot recommend this change until issues in https://github.com/astropy/astropy/issues/1977, https://github.com/scipy/scipy/issues/3227, https://github.com/astropy/astropy/pull/2294 are solved. I have submitted a PR that fixes these issues in `all_world2pix()` and provides performance improvements https://github.com/astropy/astropy/pull/2373 but it is not yet merged.

I will post here when it is safe to do this transition.

NOTE: for images that do not have distortions  `wcs.all_world2pix()` and `wcs.all_pix2world()` are similar to their -non-distortion-aware analogs `wcs.wcs_world2pix()` and `wcs.wcs_pix2world()`.

issue_title: PycurlClient does not support TCP keep-alive
issue_body:
The RequestsClient uses a session with which one is able to get TCP keep-alive by creating a single client per process:

`stripe.default_http_client = stripe.http_client.RequestsClient()`

After the initial stripe API handshake, the API calls can be completed much quicker. For long running processes (say a web-application) this yields a great speed up. In our case it cuts the costs of API calls in half.

Doing the same thing with the PycurlClient doesn't work however. The following setup will still discard the connection after every single request:

`stripe.default_http_client = stripe.http_client.PycurlClient()`

To facilitate this the Curl instance would have to be kept around if I'm not mistaken. So basically the PycurlClient might want to use curl as a kind of session, just like the RequestsClient does:

```python
class PycurlClient(object):
    def __init__(..., curl=None):
        self.curl = curl or pycurl.Curl()
    def request(...):
        curl = self.curl
```

Python Version: 3.5.3
Library Version: 1.57.1

issue_title: Updating subspacematrix error handling with VASP
issue_body:
In the custodian.vasp.handlers.py script, there is an error-handling method for the "subspacematrix" (i.e. "WARNING: Sub-Space-Matrix is not hermitian in DAV") error.  As it stands right now, the custodian file tries to change LREAL when this error occurs. I have found that in systems I have studied, this error often occurs when PREC is not set to Accurate. I have attached an example set of input files. It runs with PREC = Accurate but has the subspace error when PREC = Normal. It therefore may be desirable to add a PREC switch for this error message. Note that this may be dependent on the particular VASP build.

issue_title: Extend feature.structure_tensor to 3D
issue_body:
## Description
There should be an equivalent function to `feature.structure_tensor()` and the associated `feature.structure_tensor_eigvals()` extended to three dimensions. 

The tensor structure is described [here](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Structure_tensor#The_3D_structure_tensor), and would just involve calculating a third partial derivative and including 3 more cross terms.

issue_title: PyRegion.py `initialize` receives "dims, splitterMaps", not "inputs, outputs"
issue_body:
From `PyRegion.py`

``` python
  @abstractmethod
  def initialize(self, inputs, outputs):
    """Initialize the node after the network is fully linked
    It is called once by NuPIC before the first call to compute(). It is
    a good place to perform one time initialization that depend on the inputs
    and/or outputs. The region may also remember its inputs and outputs here
    because they will not change.
    inputs: dict of numpy arrays (one per input)
    outputs: dict of numpy arrays (one per output)
    """
```

But if you look at PyRegion.cpp, it **does not** pass in `inputs, outputs`. It passes in `dims, splitterMaps`.

Multiple regions in nupic think they're receiving `inputs, outputs` but aren't consuming them.

I'm not sure what the story is here, i.e. how it got out of sync. I think the best fix might be to pass in `inputs, outputs`, because otherwise the Python regions have no way to access inputs/outputs during initialization (whereas C++ encoders can call `getInput` and `getOutput`). It's important to be able to access inputs during initialization because, for example, the SPRegion needs to tell the Spatial Pooler the input width.

issue_title: Why does smoke-test.sh pull a fixed version of e2e.py from kinto-signer?
issue_body:
smoke-test.sh contains the following code:

```sh
curl -O https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Kinto/kinto-signer/1.3.0/scripts/e2e.py
```

Note that the version of `e2e.py` is taken from `kinto-signer` 1.3.0. However, later in the file is:

```sh
curl -O https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Kinto/kinto-signer/master/scripts/validate_signature.py
```

My questions are:

1. Why 1.3.0 instead of master? Is this meant to be a "known good" version? Is it supposed to represent existing code "in the wild"?
2. For that matter, why master instead of some "latest release"?
3. Actually, why do we `curl` these files at all rather than i.e. extracting them from installed packages?

issue_title: Add a programatic way to find out if a settings write was successful
issue_body:
Currently if a `SBP_MSG_SETTINGS_WRITE` fails in firmware, it just spits out an error log.

This does not provide a robust way for a program or script to determine if a setting was successfully written.

We need add a `SBP_MSG_SETTINGS_WRITE_RESP` that:
- returns a status code to indicate success, or an error (and a unique code for each type of failure to help a programmer find which field was bad)
- returns the new setting value if successful (or the old value if it failed), this means a tool can check for any roundtrip problems (e.g. UART corruption)

issue_title: session cookie missing from reconnect requests
issue_body:
I am currently evaluating fanout in a development environment using ngrok. I use curl to connect to my django application with a valid session:
`curl -i https://my-realm.fanoutcdn.com/eventstream/  --cookie "sessionid=my-valid-session-key"`

I have a custom manager which assigns channels based on the user. On the first request, this works fine. It seems that there are later requests which lack the session cookie to authenticate the user. If I put a breakpoint in my `get_channels_for_request` method, I can see that `request.COOKIES` contains the session id in the first request, and is an empty dictionary in later requests.

From my log files (ngxinx proxy to django runserver):
```
[11/Aug/2018:14:54:41 +0000] "GET /eventstream/ HTTP/1.1" 200 2077 "-" "curl/7.47.0" 0.322 2827 nate -  
127.0.0.1 - - [11/Aug/2018:14:54:42 +0000] "GET /eventstream/?recover=true&link=next HTTP/1.1" 200 0 "-" "-" 0.025 756 - -
127.0.0.1 - - [11/Aug/2018:14:54:43 +0000] "GET /eventstream/?recover=true&link=next HTTP/1.1" 200 0 "-" "-" 0.024 756 - -
127.0.0.1 - - [11/Aug/2018:14:56:44 +0000] "GET /eventstream/?recover=true&link=next HTTP/1.1" 200 0 "-" "-" 0.032 756 - -
127.0.0.1 - - [11/Aug/2018:14:58:45 +0000] "GET /eventstream/?recover=true&link=next HTTP/1.1" 200 0 "-" "-" 0.031 756 - -
```

On the first request, the client is getting the correct channels (and if I send an event very quickly I can see it in my terminal). On later requests the `user` in the ChannleManager is None, so the client is not subscribed to any channels. Note that when I say latter request, I am referring to my nginx logs, as the original curl request has remained open.

This appears to make it impossible to have user-specific channels.

issue_title: uploader: migrate tasks from legacy BibUpload
issue_body:
## Idea
Replace the current BibUpload.
## Proposal
Create a new uploader keeping this list of ideas about it in mind:
* Work with JSON natively rather than MARC, although the last could be uploaded as well.
* Run tasks in parallel when possible, for example one huge insert coming from one file could generate several insert tasks that could run in parallel.
* Easy to customise depending on the installation needs. For that we thought about creating *lightweight workflows* (https://github.com/romanchyla/workflow) for each of the common/current modes, so each installation could override the current ones or implement their owns.
* This lightweight workflows should be made out of small monolithic tasks and it would be nice to have a rollback function for each of this functions in case of error in any of them while running the workflow (reverse workflow?)
* Plug the new documents module with a default document storage (same behaviour as BibDocFile).
* Fire signals before and after running any workflow would be a good thing to "connect" the new uploader with other Invenio tools like `bibindex`, `webcoll`, etc.

## Tasks

- [x] insert mode
- [ ] replace mode 
- [ ] correct mode

--
see implementation at #1850

issue_title: Date ranges can't cross year boundaries
issue_body:
Originally

Example:
```
year:2008-02->2008-06
```
works ok.

However
```
year:2008-02->2009-02
```
yields the same results as
```
year:2008-02->2009
```

For the moment I suggest working around by splitting your date search in twain (range to end of year, range from beginning of next).  But this should be repaired.

issue_title: Inspire xmlpublic
issue_body:
Originally by *annetteh* on 2012-04-26

The spires xmlpublic format needs to be reproduced, see e.g.
http://www.slac.stanford.edu/spires/find/hep/www?rawcmd=fin+a+hawking&FORMAT=xmlpublic&SEQUENCE=

specifically (https://rt.inspirehep.net/Ticket/Display.html?id=180611):
- Title
- Author list (separated authors, so it can be automatically analyzed)
- Journal (if published)
- Volume (if published)
- Pages (if published)
- Year
- ArXiv/eprint number + report nr (if present)
- Citation count (this is very very helpful)
- Unique INSPIRE key
- DOI
and more (conf info...)

issue_title: oauth: disconnect failing
issue_body:
Disconnecting an account currently fails because the id provided from `current_user.get_id` [here](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-github/blob/901f396c191a6e50b62eecd6628e7aca1d327174/invenio_github/handlers.py#L75) is a string and not an integer (`'1234'`, not `1234`), making future passing as an argument faulty. Since this is a common issue throughout views and other APIs, a better solution would be to perform a cast to `int` at the [model's method](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-github/blob/901f396c191a6e50b62eecd6628e7aca1d327174/invenio_github/models.py#L192) like:

```python
repo.user_id != user_id

repo.user_id != int(user_id)
```

issue_title: global: make beta release
api: allow partial validation
issue_body:
Currently the record validation prevents to save a record that is not 100% compliant with the schema.

It would be nice to allow partial validation within the context of, for example, deposit, so a user can save a "dirty" record, get back the errors it might have and, at some point in time, fix the errors and save the record again.

On possible option to do that is to, after trying to validate with the general schema, remove all the `required` constrains from it and try to save again (keeping the formats and types intact)

An alternative approach could be, keep only formats and type constrains in the schema and remove everything else for the partial validation. 

--

cc @inveniosoftware/triagers

issue_title: Error in unpickling DateTime object having '+NNNN' timezone
issue_body:
Hi,

We cannot unpickle the pickled result of a DateTime object having '+NNNN' timezone.

With 4.1, it is possible for +0300, but not possible for +0430 (Afghanistan timezone).

```
>>> from DateTime import DateTime
>>> a=DateTime('2007/01/02 12:34:56.789 +0300')
>>> a._tz
'GMT+3'
>>> a._timezone_naive
False
>>> a.tzoffset()
10800
>>> b = DateTime()
>>> b.__setstate__(a.__getstate__())
>>> b._tz
'GMT+3'
>>> a == b
True
>>> a=DateTime('2007/01/02 12:34:56.789 +0430')
>>> a._tz
'+0430'
>>> a._timezone_naive
True
>>> a.tzoffset()
16200
>>> b = DateTime()
>>> b.__setstate__(a.__getstate__())
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/path/to/DateTime/DateTime.py", line 458, in __setstate__
    self._parse_args(value[0], value[2])
  File "/path/to/DateTime/DateTime.py", line 789, in _parse_args
    tz = _TZINFO._zmap[tz.lower()]
KeyError: '+0430'
```

With 3.0.3, it is not possible even for +0300.

```
>>> from DateTime import DateTime
>>> a=DateTime('2007/01/02 12:34:56.789 +0300')
>>> a._tz
'+0300'
>>> a._timezone_naive
True
>>> a.__getstate__()
(1167730496.789, True, '+0300')
>>> a.tzoffset()
10800
>>> b = DateTime()
>>> b.__setstate__(a.__getstate__())
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<console>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/path/to/DateTime/DateTime.py", line 455, in __setstate__
    self._parse_args(value[0], value[2])
  File "/path/to/DateTime/DateTime.py", line 787, in _parse_args
    tz = _TZINFO._zmap[tz.lower()]
KeyError: '+0300'
```

With DateTime 2.12.8, it is possible for both +0300 and +0430.

```
>>> from DateTime import DateTime
>>> a = DateTime('2007/01/02 12:34:56.789 +0300')
>>> a._tz
'+0300'
>>> a._timezone_naive
True
>>> a.tzoffset()
10800
>>> b = DateTime()
>>> b.__setstate__(a.__dict__) # using __dict__ as there is not __getstate__() in 2.12.8
>>> b._tz
'+0300'
>>> a == b
True
>>> a = DateTime('2007/01/02 12:34:56.789 +0430')
>>> a._tz
'+0430'
>>> a._timezone_naive
True
>>> a.tzoffset()
16200
>>> b = DateTime()
>>> b.__setstate__(a.__dict__) # using __dict__ as there is not __getstate__() in 2.12.8
>>> b._tz
'+0430'
>>> a == b
True
```

issue_title: Add support for HTTP basic auth
issue_body:
In some places we need to be able to programmatically bypass the perimeter - e.g. when running automated tests, or certain remote API calls. Adding basic auth would enable this.

NB Basic Auth transmits credentials in plain text, so should only be used with HTTPS, and even then should not be used to secure anything that requires strong security. It's for basic pre-launch perimeters only.

issue_title: Pagination link does not meet accessibility compliant to screen reader users
issue_body:
Recently one of our 4.x plone sites was audited for WCAG 2.0 AA. The report came back and mentioned that pagination links in faceted navigation need to have hidden structural labels, 'Go to page:'. Below are explanation on 'hidden structural labels' from the agent.

Hidden structural labels are specific text for screen reader users only, which describe the content. Nested lists are hierarchical, but their hierarchy is not clear to screen reader users. Therefore you need to use a hidden structural label to indicate the beginning and end of sub-navigation. For code examples see: http://maxdesign.com.au/articles/about-structural-labels/

For examples where hidden structural labels have been employed in navigation see:
http://www.adcq.qld.gov.au/about-us/the-commissioner (left-hand navigation)
http://www.melbourne.vic.gov.au/business/doing-business/Pages/doing-business-in-melbourne.aspx

issue_title: Allow users to filter in/out some board
issue_body:
while some users might appreciate the overall status for all boards at a specific commit, there are developers who will be interested in trends/status over time/builds for a specific board (or set of boards).

right now it is hard to look at results/data for a specific board when navigating on qa-reports. 

in fact it is even not obivous on which platform tests were run, e.g. if you go to 

https://qa-reports.linaro.org/lkft/linux-mainline-oe/build/v4.16-rc5-4-gfc6eabbbf8ef/

it would be nice to have 'boards' in metadata, and if we could click on a board and filter out only relevant information for this board.. e.g. display only lava tests for this board, only results for it.. 

and we could do it similarly at the 'project' level, e.g. https://qa-reports.linaro.org/lkft/linux-mainline-oe/, it could list all boards that have been used in this project...

issue_title: Options quotes are missing timezone
issue_body:
I ran into this problem on the 'Quote_Time' field when pulling options quotes using `pandas.io.data.Options.get_all_data()`. While 'Quote_Time' is objectively 'US/Eastern' timezone, it actually shows up in the dataframe as naive timezone, which in my use case then got interpreted as UTC. I would suggest setting the timezone correctly when the data is pulled from Yahoo.

I'm pretty sure that the Expiry field in the index also uses a naive timezone (`= None`).

[Here](https://github.com/aisthesis/pynance/issues/3) is a related issue for further clarification.

issue_title: Consistent behavior from errors in setup and teardown
issue_body:
Currently we have the following

Exception in `setup_class`: Extra result entry, mark test cases as skipped
Exception in `setup_test`: Mark test as error using details
Exception in `teardown_test`: Mark test as error using extra_errors
Exception in `teardown_class`: No record

I propose the following for the sake of consistency (both with pyunit and with self):

Exception in `setup_class`:
    No extra result entry, mark test cases as skipped, add to extra_errors for each result
Exception in `setup_test`: Either
    1)  mark test case result as skipped and use extra_errors for error message
    2)  mark test case as error and use details
Exception in `teardown_test`: Either (while being consistent with `setup_test`)
    3)  Do not change the test case result (leave it as is) and use `extra_errors` for error message
    4)  mark test case as error and use `details`
Exception in `teardown_class`:
    Do not change the test case results, add to `extra_errors` for all non-skipped results

issue_title: Make it easier to access the zphot record in the COSMOS catalog
issue_body:
@rearmstr asked about accessing the redshift information that is in the COSMOS catalog for use with an NFWHalo simulation (which uses the redshift of each galaxy to compute the correct amount of lensing).

It doesn't seem like there is currently any easy way to do this either in config (his use case) or even using the regular COSMOSCatalog class.  It requires a bit of insider knowledge of the record attribute and such.  

Anyway, here is the custom ValueType code that I proposed to access this value from a config file:

```
def COSMOS_ZPhot(config, base, value_type):
    cosmos_cat = galsim.config.GetInputObj('cosmos_catalog', config, base, 'COSMOS_ZPhot')
    # Requires that galaxy uses an explicit index.
    # This will raise an exception if index is not set.
    index = galsim.config.GetCurrentValue('gal.index', base, value_type=int)
    record = cosmos_cat.getParametricRecord(index)
    return record['zphot']
```
However, it requires that the gal object explicitly set the index to be used.  It can be simply `type : Random`, which will approximate the effect of not using an explicit type, just without the optional weight scheme for selecting an appropriate galaxy.

I propose the following updates in this issue:
* Put something like the above code into GalSim-proper, so as not to require a custom type to do this
* Have the regular COSMOSGalaxy code store the index that gets used somewhere, whether it is set via an explicit `index` parameter or not (including if the weights are used).
* Update the above code to use the stored index value, rather than using `GetCurrentValue`.
* Maybe also add a few other accessors.  E.g. flux, half-light-radius, others?
* Probably also make the config code not require the galaxy type to be COSMOSGalaxy in order to use these accessors.
* Add this as to the demo9 example.  It is currently our example for InclinedExponential, but it would be nice if we could take the size, flux, and redshift from the COSMOS catalog using the above new accessors.

issue_title: better `__repr__` for Image and deviates
issue_body: The current GalSim policy for having `eval(repr(obj))` being able to recreate any object is great for interrogating most types, but cumbersome for images and random deviates, where the repr string will quickly explode a terminal screen.  This issue is to propose that we truncate the repr for Image and deviate types so they don't totally fill the screen, (although still printing the first few array elements, similar to `ndarray.__repr__,` would still be useful for debugging).  The python standard library has a repr module that may be helpful for this.

issue_title: RandomWalk much slower when sheared
issue_body:
The following code runs 10 times faster when no shear is applied.
```python
import numpy as np
import galsim
import time

import argparse

parser=argparse.ArgumentParser()
parser.add_argument('ntrial',type=int)
parser.add_argument('--shear',action='store_true')


class Sim(object):
    def __init__(self, shear=False):
        self.shear=shear
        self.dim=100

        self.flux = 100.0
        self.hlr = 4.0
        self.n_knots=100
        self.psf=galsim.Gaussian(fwhm=0.9)

    def make_im(self):
        obj=self.get_model()
        if self.shear:
            g1,g2=self.get_shape()
            obj = obj.shear(g1=g1,g2=g2)

        obj=galsim.Convolve(obj, self.psf)
        return obj.drawImage(
            scale=0.263,
        ).array

    def get_shape(self):
        while True:
            g1,g2 = np.random.normal(scale=0.2,size=2)
            g=np.sqrt(g1**2 + g2**2)
            if g < 0.99:
                break
        return g1,g2

    def get_model(self):
        profile=galsim.Exponential(
            flux=self.flux,
            half_light_radius=self.hlr,
        )

        obj=galsim.RandomWalk(
            self.n_knots,
            profile=profile,
        )
        return obj

args=parser.parse_args()
sim=Sim(shear=args.shear)

tm=time.time()
for i in range(args.ntrial):
    im=sim.make_im()

print("time:",time.time()-tm)
```

The profiler indicates that it is spending a lot of time in transform.py  Is this something we could optimize?  This is pure peculation, but these are point sources (a delta function is used for each knot) so we should be able to do something smart to avoid this overhead it seems.

issue_title: config verbosity for COSMOSCatalog
issue_body:
I want to recommend that config by default (i.e., for the default verbosity level) say a little more about what it's doing with galaxies drawn from COSMOSCatalogs.

In particular, I have had multiple issues (like #802) where it would have been really helpful if the default verbosity level would issue a single statement at the start of a given file saying (a) exactly what catalog is being used and (b) whether the galaxies are real or parametric.

For example, in #802, I had used a template with parametric galaxies, then had the .yaml file later modify to use real galaxies, except it quietly ignored that.  It took me ages before I realized it was not in fact using real galaxies.  I had to go all the way up to verbosity=3, and then notice that it was calling the galaxy generator for parametric galaxies.  If the default verbosity had a single statement at the start of making each large image saying what COSMOSCatalog was used, and how it's being used, I think that would make debugging simple without adding too much clutter to the outputs.

Thoughts?

issue_title: Using results of Poisson CCD Simulator to simulate BF effect
issue_body: Craig Lage at UC Davis has developed a Poisson equation solver that computes the distortion of the pixel edges as the electrons get collected in the CCD pixels. In the DESC Hack day today, we made some progress towards integrating his code (in C++) with the C++ layer of the GalSim. We identified  that the natural way to include this functionality would be by tweaking the photon-shooting method inside the `drawImage` routine.  I will push the code after some cleanup, but this is not completely done yet.

issue_title: Is there no support for api/util/status?
reset_db causes NotAuthorized
issue_body:
I'm using `ckanapi.LocalCKAN` for the tests of one of our CKAN extensions, and I'm running into problems when I call [`ckan.tests.helpers.reset_db`](https://github.com/ckan/ckan/blob/05685a3aeda49d27213c5727e78068ffad248efd/ckan/tests/helpers.py#L46) inbetween test cases: afterwards, some (but not all) action function calls made via ckanapi raise `NotAuthorized`.

Here is a script that reproduces the issue for me (you can probably ignore the setup of the CKAN environment, it's required to be able to run this in a standalone script). The script expects the path of a CKAN INI as a parameter.

```python
from ckan.tests.helpers import reset_db

import ckanapi


#
# BEGIN SETUP OF CKAN ENV
#

# Adapted from ckanext-archiver
def _load_ckan_environment(ini_path):
    '''
    Load CKAN environment.
    '''
    ini_path = os.path.abspath(ini_path)
    logging.config.fileConfig(ini_path, disable_existing_loggers=False)
    conf = paste.deploy.appconfig('config:' + ini_path)
    load_environment(conf.global_conf, conf.local_conf)
    _register_translator()


# Adapted from ckanext-archiver
def _register_translator():
    '''
    Register a translator in this thread.
    '''
    global registry
    try:
        registry
    except NameError:
        registry = Registry()
    registry.prepare()
    global translator_obj
    try:
        translator_obj
    except NameError:
        translator_obj = MockTranslator()
    registry.register(translator, translator_obj)

_load_ckan_environment(sys.argv[1])

#
# END SETUP OF CKAN ENV
#


def create_pkg_and_res(ckan, name):
    pkg = ckan.action.package_create(name=name)
    res = ckan.action.resource_create(package_id=pkg['id'], url='bar')

ckan = ckanapi.LocalCKAN()

create_pkg_and_res(ckan, 'foo1')  # Works
reset_db()
create_pkg_and_res(ckan, 'foo2')  # Fails with NotAuthorized
```

The first call to `create_pkg_and_res` works as expected, but the second one (after the `reset_db`) raises `NotAuthorized` when calling `resource_create`:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "./notauthorized.py", line 67, in <module>
    create_pkg_and_res(ckan, 'foo2')  # Fails with NotAuthorized
  File "./notauthorized.py", line 61, in create_pkg_and_res
    res = ckan.action.resource_create(package_id=pkg['id'], url='bar')
  File "/usr/lib/ckan/default/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/ckanapi/common.py", line 51, in action
    return self._ckan.call_action(name, data_dict=kwargs)
  File "/usr/lib/ckan/default/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/ckanapi/localckan.py", line 71, in call_action
    return self._get_action(action)(context, data_dict)
  File "/var/transparenz.karlsruhe.de/ckan/ckan/logic/__init__.py", line 457, in wrapped
    result = _action(context, data_dict, **kw)
  File "/var/transparenz.karlsruhe.de/ckan/ckan/logic/action/create.py", line 292, in resource_create
    _check_access('resource_create', context, data_dict)
  File "/var/transparenz.karlsruhe.de/ckan/ckan/logic/__init__.py", line 299, in check_access
    raise NotAuthorized(msg)
ckan.logic.NotAuthorized: <function resource_create at 0x7fb48e0eeaa0> requires an authenticated user
```

Interestingly, the second call to `package_create` does *not* raise an exception.

When the `reset_db` call is removed, both calls to `create_pkg_and_res` succeed.

I'm running CKAN 2.7.2 and ckanapi 4.1.

issue_title: Disconnecting while MQTTClient.deliver_message() is pending raises IndexError
issue_body:
If the broker disconnects while `MQTTClient.deliver_message()` is pending and `auto_reconnect` is turned off (and presumably also if `auto_reconnect` fails, but I haven't tested that), `deliver_message()` will raise an `IndexError`:

    WARNING:hbmqtt.client:Disconnected from broker
    DEBUG:transitions.core:Initiating transition from state connected to state disconnected...
    DEBUG:transitions.core:Exiting state connected. Processing callbacks...
    INFO:transitions.core:Exited state connected
    DEBUG:transitions.core:Entering state disconnected. Processing callbacks...
    INFO:transitions.core:Entered state disconnected
    ERROR:asyncio:Task exception was never retrieved
    future: <Task finished coro=<mqtt_loop() done, defined at ./foo.py:19> exception=IndexError('pop from an empty deque',)>
    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "./foo.py", line 25, in mqtt_loop
        message = await mqtt.deliver_message()
      File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/hbmqtt/client.py", line 334, in deliver_message
        self.client_tasks.pop()
    IndexError: pop from an empty deque

This happens because `cancel_tasks()` called by `handle_connection_close()` pops all tasks from `self.client_tasks` as it cancels them. When the `deliver_task` is cancelled, `deliver_message()` processes it as if it finished normally and tries to pop it from `self.client_tasks` again, resulting in the exception.

I'm not sure what the intended behavior here is. From a user perspective, I think it'd be most useful if `deliver_message()` would just pass on the `ClientException("Connection lost")` from `cancel_tasks()`.

issue_title: Simplify period management
issue_body:
## Observations

Looking at `openfisca-france`, it seems that most of the variables have a natural period granularity. For instance, the `rsa` is defined for a month, while `irpp` defined for a year.

## Current situation

The way this natural period is set for a given formula is today complex and confusing. For instance : 
```py
class rsa(Variable):
    [...]

    def function(famille, period):
        period = period.this_month
        [...]
        return period, result

```
The current implementation allows a formula to return a result for a different period than the one it has been called for.

For instance, if I call `rsa` for `2015`, the first line`period = period.this_month` will change `period` to `2015-01`. This period will be returned with the result. It is then the openfisca middleware that will check that the returned period is the same than the required one. In our case, it will throw an error except if a certain option has been set for the calculation.

### Problems caused
- Returning the period with the result is not natural, and often forgotten.
- The first line, which defines the natural period of the formula, is really unclear.
- The first line is repeated in almost all formulas.

## Proposition
If each variable has a natural period, then a **declarative approach** seems more simple.

### Example
Our previous formula becomes : 
```py
class rsa(Variable):
    period = MONTH # keyword to be discussed
    [...]

    def function(famille, period):
        [...]
        return result

```

### Behaviour
- If I call a variable for a period that is **not its natural one**, **an error is raised** before entering the formula.
    - For instance if I call `rsa` for `2015`, or `irpp` for `2015-01`.
- If a formula has no natural period, we do not check anything (some variables are period agnostics, for instance inputs or basis input combinations)

issue_title: What do you think about making it more flexible?
issue_body:
This is an awesome project! Congrats! It'a amazing how complete it is.

For me it would be nice to be able to use the queryinspect programatically instead of coupling it to my project and making it depending on settings.

I teach a lot and work on many projects that need refactoring and performance improvements. So I think it would be great to use the inspector without the middleware.

I have some rudimentary solution for what I need with the `logdb` function fo [sqlformatter](https://github.com/henriquebastos/sqlformatter). [You can see the source at here](https://github.com/henriquebastos/sqlformatter/blob/master/sqlformatter.py).

Be decoupling the inspector from the predetermined workflow of a middleware one could:
- use as a view decorator
- use as a contextmanager
- turn it on on the beginning of a process and off somewhere else.
- easily integrate with other tools like django-debugtoolbar and django-devserver

If you feel this is aligned with your vision, I can do some work on that.

Again, congrats and thanks for sharing.

issue_title: Bug: `chalice deploy` fails to discover required permissions for Lambda IAM role
issue_body:
Ordinarily, `chalice deploy` finds IAM permissions required for the application to work, builds an IAM role and prompts the user for authorization to add necessary permissions to the role:

```
$ chalice deploy
Regen deployment package.

The following actions will be added to the execution policy:

s3:GetObject

Would you like to continue?  [Y/n]:
```

However, in some situations, the required permissions are not discovered and the app is deployed without the required permissions, resulting in botocore.exceptions.ClientError (AccessDenied) when a request is received.

In the example below, the app is deployed with a role that is missing the permissions that it requires.

### MVCE
**app.py:**
```
import boto3
from chalice import Chalice

app = Chalice(app_name='bug')
s3 = boto3.client('s3')
app.debug = True


@app.route('/')
def index():
    data = []
    for key in ('foo', 'bar'):
        data.append(get_content(key))
    return {'data': data}


def get_content(key):
    content = s3.get_object(Bucket='this-chalice-resembles-a-grail',
                            Key=key)['Body'].read()
    return content
```

issue_title: Document current glyph name syntax
issue_body: According to [§2.f.i of the OpenType Feature File syntax](http://www.adobe.com/devnet/opentype/afdko/topic_feature_file_syntax.html#2.f.i), glyph names must not contain hyphens. However, @readroberts wrote in https://github.com/fonttools/fonttools/issues/559#issuecomment-224007849 that makeotf has been changed to accept glyph names with `- + * : ~ ^ !`. This was done so that makeotf could support workflows where features get compiled on fonts that have “development” glyph names. Could the spec be amended to reflect what’s currently accepted? The spec might still want to contain a warning that when a font uses these characters, it will contain invalid PostScript and Type 1 names.

issue_title: DISCUSSION: Allow http as an argument to Connection so libraries other than httplib2 can be used
issue_body:
This way we can support people using `Twisted` (for example) or other frameworks without having to write any other code. Though docs could be nice? We could supply an abstract class for them to subclass?

----

As of 12ac983fe613785c998aeeb1d8b981f4b5b347d9:

```
$ git log -1 --pretty=%H
12ac983fe613785c998aeeb1d8b981f4b5b347d9
$ git grep '\.http' -- gcloud/ | egrep -v 'test'
gcloud/datastore/connection.py:        :raises: :class:`six.moves.http_client.HTTPException` if the response
gcloud/datastore/connection.py:        headers, content = self.http.request(
gcloud/datastore/connection.py:            raise six.moves.http_client.HTTPException(message)
gcloud/storage/connection.py:        return self.http.request(uri=url, method=method, headers=headers,
gcloud/storage/key.py:        download.InitializeDownload(request, self.connection.http)
gcloud/storage/key.py:        upload.InitializeUpload(request, conn.http)
gcloud/storage/key.py:            http_wrapper.MakeRequest(conn.http, request, retries=num_retries)
```

We only actively use the `httplib2.Http()` instance twice. Once in `storage.connection.Connection.make_request`:

```python
        return self.http.request(uri=url, method=method, headers=headers,
                                 body=data)
```

and once in `datastore.connection.Connection._request`:

```python
        headers, content = self.http.request(
            uri=self.build_api_url(dataset_id=dataset_id, method=method),
            method='POST', headers=headers, body=data)
```

----

In either case the object only needs to be able to accept

- `uri`
- `method`
- `headers`
- `body`

and needs to [append][4] a

```
Authorization: Bearer ya29.some-OAUTH-TOKEN...
```

header to `headers`. (See the [source][3], the user agent is also updated and the request is retried if a [401 Unauthorized][5] is encountered, but an external client need not do either of those.)

----

ASIDE: Should we add an auth label?

[1]: https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/gcloud-python/blob/12ac983fe613785c998aeeb1d8b981f4b5b347d9/gcloud/storage/connection.py#L259
[2]: https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/gcloud-python/blob/12ac983fe613785c998aeeb1d8b981f4b5b347d9/gcloud/datastore/connection.py#L65
[3]: https://github.com/google/oauth2client/blob/2864ebc7a52832f0fda3820d6aeb8723c0d09597/oauth2client/client.py#L525-L556
[4]: https://github.com/google/oauth2client/blob/2864ebc7a52832f0fda3820d6aeb8723c0d09597/oauth2client/client.py#L584-L590
[5]: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_HTTP_status_codes#4xx_Client_Error

issue_title: Should 'pubsub.subscription.Subscription.pull()' support auto-acknowledge
issue_body:
In pull mode, the subscriber is responsible for sending an `acknowledge` request after fetching messages via `pull`.  While separating the two allows for some advanced usage (e.g., to allow "peeking" at the queue, or to allow retries after certain kinds of errors), some applications might find having to manage the manual `acknowledge` burdensome.

Should we add a boolean `acknowledge_immediately` argument to `Subscription.pull()`?  If so, what should its default value be?

issue_title: Cloud spanner client does not seem to be retrying broken streams
issue_labels: [{'id': 539494117, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/api:%20spanner', 'name': 'api: spanner', 'color': 'cd7f32', 'default': False}, {'id': 544604244, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/priority:%20p1', 'name': 'priority: p1', 'color': 'ffa03e', 'default': False}, {'id': 486182624, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/status:%20release%20blocking', 'name': 'status: release blocking', 'color': 'ffee58', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
[Documentation](http://google-cloud-python.readthedocs.io/en/latest/spanner/snapshot-usage.html) for snapshot transactions mentions:

```
If streaming a chunk fails due to a “resumable” error, Session.read() retries the StreamingRead API reqeust, passing the resume_token from the last partial result streamed.
```

This is the correct thing to do but the client actually does not seem to be doing this. I looked around a little bit in the code and I could find anyplace where it is retrying the read with the resume token. 
This is the place where the stream would throw an error if it broke:
https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/9d9b6c0708ae6553458ad6107e5ad8efb23762e8/spanner/google/cloud/spanner/streamed.py#L132

 I dont see any exception handling happening here and it looks like that the error would just be propagated back to the user.

```
java.lang.NoSuchMethodError: com.google.common.util.concurrent.MoreExecutors.directExecutor()Ljava/util/concurrent/Executor;
	at com.google.api.gax.retrying.BasicRetryingFuture.<init>(BasicRetryingFuture.java:77)

issue_title: IE breadcrumb display is ugly when using SASS version 3.4
issue_body:
When using the latest version of SASS (version 3.4) with bootstrap-sass, the breadcrumb separator is wrongly encoded as ISO-8859-1 when using Internet Explorer version 10 and 11.  

The problematic character seems to be the Unicode non-breaking space (\00a0) on this line:
https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap-sass/blob/master/assets/stylesheets/bootstrap/_breadcrumbs.scss#L17

In SASS version 3.3, the hexcode for the Unicode non-breaking space was left as-is in the final CSS. So, the final CSS would actually include "\00a0".  However, in SASS version 3.4, this behavior has changed, and in the final CSS the character is now translated to a Unicode space (" ").

While most browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Opera) properly display this new Unicode space (" ") from CSS, for some reason Internet Explorer wants to [read it as ISO-8859-1 and displays "Â " instead](http://stackoverflow.com/a/1462039/3750035). The resulting breadcrumb is something like this (assuming your breadcrumb-separator is the default slash):

    Home /Â  Page /Â  SubPage

In the end, this means that SASS version 3.4 + bootstrap-sass results in ugly breadcrumbs in Internet Explorer. Whereas, SASS version 3.3 + bootstrap-sass works perfectly in all browsers.

More information (including some possible workarounds using functions) on this new behavior in SASS version 3.4 can be found in this related SASS ticket/request: https://github.com/sass/sass/issues/1395

The only workaround I've found so far is to simply revert to using SASS version 3.3.

issue_title: Mismatch in Graph.resolve_address behaviour between v1 and v2
issue_body:
In `v1`, the `def resolve_address` method does not inject to the Graph, but in `v2` it currently does. This results in some subtle behaviour changes around bootstrapping, which resolves targets using its `get_alternate_target_roots` method.

When `resolve_address` injects to the graph, the results is that those targets are guaranteed to be in the context (and fetched by ivy, for example) even when they're not in use.

issue_title: Should we manage product_types in a global space?
issue_body:
Product_types is the key to do cross tasks communication, now most tasks just use a text constant which not a proper way to enforce or discover the product_types. In my mind, product_types is in the same category as mime, http headers, which helps if we have a global place for the standard ones, and also leaves some slots for customization.

Not sure whether it is overkill though :)

issue_title: BuildConfiguration should warn on override of aliases using a global namespace.
issue_body: Currently BuildConfiguration checks each alias type individually against only that type's aliases; ie it checks objects for collisions with other objects, but not for collisions with targets, etc.  Since the aliases are all merged into a single global namespace for the BUILD parse, the checking should likewise be against the same global namespace.

>The model completer is also available via the .completer property on shellcomplete.AWSShellCompleter. I think I still have some refactoring to do to get all the various autocompleters more coherent.

issue_title: Race condition when multiple gunicorn worker download models with same name
issue_body:
In our docker container, when multiple gunicorn worker download same models from S3 to disk, there will be read-write race condition because models are with the same name.

My proposal to fix it is adding a new optional argument: extract-to (Recommendation?) where the model will be saved and extracted to. 
Then we can create directory including gunicorn worker pid so models are separate across workers. It won't affect our current local serving flow but just requires some doc changes.
This argument would also be helpful if someone want to extract the model in different location rather than cwd.

issue_title: Insert MathML as plain text for Word 2010 to render it as an equation
issue_body:
Hi everybody,

I am trying to insert some MathML code into a *.docx document. To do so, I do the following:

```
#!/bin/env python
# -*- coding: utf8 -*-

from docx import Document


doc = Document('template.docx')

# Word needs a specific XML namespace in the '<math>' tag in order to embed MathML text into a math area
# Also, the MathML expression must contain no newline or Word will create one distinct math area for each line
math_ml = '<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>2</mn></mfrac></math>'

# Insert the MathML text into Word
doc.add_paragraph().add_run(text=math_ml, style=None).font.math = True
doc.save('test.docx')
```

When I open the resulting document in Word 2010, [this is what I see](http://image.noelshack.com/fichiers/2016/38/1474546928-word.jpg).
The math area has been correctly created, but the code itself has not been rendered.

The interesting thing is when I open the resulting document and then [copy](http://image.noelshack.com/fichiers/2016/38/1474547629-word.jpg) then [paste](http://image.noelshack.com/fichiers/2016/38/1474547168-word.jpg) the MathML code from/to the math area using "Paste as plain text" directly from Word UI, it gets [properly rendered](http://image.noelshack.com/fichiers/2016/38/1474547408-word.jpg).

I have come to the conclusion that when a style is applied to the MathML text, then Word does not render it as an equation. And this assertion is verified by the fact that when you use another paste option like "Keep source formatting" or "Melt both formats", then the equation is not rendered using Word UI.

My question is: **how can I insert the MathML text as plain text into Word without any style using python-docx lib ?**
I tried those, but none of them worked:

```
doc.add_paragraph().add_run(text=math_ml, style=None).font.math = True
doc.add_paragraph().add_run(text=math_ml, style='').font.math = True
doc.add_paragraph().add_run(text=math_ml).font.math = True
```

Or maybe this is currently not supported by this library ?

Thanks for your help :)

issue_title: Names of functions
issue_body:
Early on we decided on `imread` and `imsave` because these seemed to be the most used names in related software. However, we now have `imageio.save` returning ` Writer` object, which seems inconsistent.

I propose to have `imwrite` `mimwrite` `volwrite`, and keep `imsave` as an alias. The `mimsave` and friends shall be deprecated and then removed in a later release.

Further, `writer = imageio.write(...)` feels a bit weird, because you might expect that someting is written at the moment that write is called. So mayber `writer = imageio.Writer(...)` makes more sense (even if its a factory function rather than a class). 

Opinions?

cc @rossant

issue_title: update breadcrumbs usage guidance
issue_body:
In a recent change related to granular import/export, we added a new prop to the core breadcrumbs component: 

```
+      /**
 +       * When set to 'true', a breadcrumb will be shown for each item in 'items' array.
 +       * Otherwise, the first item will be omitted.
 +       */
 +      showAllCrumbs: {
 +        type: Boolean,
 +        default: false,
 +      },
```

https://github.com/learningequality/kolibri/pull/2610/files#diff-d37333989ca3443ceed46359f1fab1feR113 

We need to update the style guide to give guidance on when this prop is intended to be used.

cc @khangmach @jtamiace @jonboiser @christianmemije

issue_title: Feature Request - User Accessibility Settings
issue_body:
Feature Request - User Accessibility Settings - opening for Kolibri as [original KA Lite issue](https://github.com/learningequality/ka-lite/issues/3921) is not going to happen within the scope of KA Lite.

#### Examples of possible options
- [ ] **High Contrast & Background** (see the [original issue](https://github.com/learningequality/ka-lite/issues/3921) for screenshots) @jtamiace @66eli77
It would be awesome to have, but if the contrast is setup properly from the get go, can be an enhancement feature. 

- [ ] **Show only videos with available subtitles** (there was an original issue at some point?)
Very useful for DHOH (deaf and hard on hearing) who **must have** subtitles.

- [ ] **Show videos in English with subtitles in user's target language**
See the [original KA Lite issue](https://github.com/learningequality/ka-lite/issues/3913) for details. In KA Lite this is valid for DHOH users who would use non-English UI, but would be better served if they view the original video in English, with the translated subs in their own language. Implementation for other channels would depend on how the video content is transcribed & translated.

- [ ] More to come...

issue_title: Only initial value of initial_requires is used
issue_body:
If the value of `install_requires` comes from another variable, and that variable is updated later in the code, the requirements are not fully detected, eg:

```
requires = []
requires.add('Django==1.5.5')

setup(
   install_requires=requires
   ...
)
```

Example: https://github.com/sigmavirus24/github3.py/blob/a728fffa49e0da2a404ee2c8651b2f0eb3579cb7/setup.py
